spec_match.rationale: Uses the spec's close-request path correctly: requestClose() fires a cancellable cancel event first, and beforetoggle then reports the open-to-closed transition when the close proceeds.

interactive.rationale: Chrome DevTools MCP exercised open, request close, dirty text input, cancel, keep-open confirmation, and discard flows; the dialog remained open while dirty until discard was confirmed.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Feature card now frames the page as a requestClose() and cancel-event guard, matching the repaired concept.

edge_cases.rationale: The live log now shows clean close ordering as cancel -> beforetoggle(cancelable=false) -> toggle -> close, and dirty close ordering as cancel.preventDefault() with no close transition until the discard branch.

design_system.rationale: CSS variables, form controls follow design system.

summary: The close guard now uses requestClose() and the cancel event for the cancellable close-request hook, while beforetoggle is logged as the subsequent close-transition notification. Chrome DevTools MCP verified clean close ordering, dirty cancel prevention, discard close, no console errors, no failed local requests, and /v131/dialog-toggle-events/conformance/?verify=after-event-order passing 8/8 assertions.
